return them to Vault.\n     */\n    function withdrawAll() external;\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Returns the current balance of the given asset.\n     */\n    function checkBalance(address _asset)\n external view returns (bool);\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Collect reward tokens from the Strategy.\n     */\n    function collectRewardTokens() external;\n\n    /**\n     * @dev The address array of the reward tokens for the Strategy.\n     */\n    function getRewardTokenAddresses() external view returns (address[] memory);\n\n    function harvesterAddress() external view returns (address);\n\n    function transferToken(address token, uint256 amount) external;\n\n    function setRewardTokenAddresses(address[] calldata _rewardTokenAddresses)\n        external;\n}\n","keccak256":"0x79ca47defb3b5a56bba13f14c440838152fd1c1aa640476154516a16da4da8ba","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/interfaces/IVault.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\nimport { VaultStorage } from \"../vault/VaultStorage.sol\";\n\ninterface IVault {\n    // slither-disable-start constable-states\n\n    event AssetAllocated(address _asset, address _strategy, uint256 _amount);\n    event StrategyApproved(address _addr);\n    event StrategyRemoved(address _addr);\n    event Mint(address _addr, uint256 _value);\n    event Redeem(address _addr, uint256 _value);\n    event CapitalPaused();\n    event CapitalUnpaused();\n    event DefaultStrategyUpdated(address _strategy);\n    event RebasePaused();\n    event RebaseUnpaused();\n    event VaultBufferUpdated(uint256 _vaultBuffer);\n    event AllocateThresholdUpdated(uint256 _threshold);\n    event StrategistUpdated(address _address);\n    event indexed _requestId,\n        uint256 _amount,\n        uint256 _queued\n    );\n    event WithdrawalClaimed(\n        address indexed _withdrawer,\n        uint256 indexed _requestId,\n        uint256 _amount\n    );\n    event WithdrawalClaimable(uint256 _claimable, uint256 _newClaimable);\n    event WithdrawalClaimDelayUpdated(uint256 _newDelay);\n\n    // Governable.sol\n    function view\n        returns (VaultStorage.Strategy memory);\n\n    /// @notice Deprecated: use `asset()` instead.\n    function isSupportedAsset(address _asset) external view returns (bool);\n\n    function function withdrawalQueueMetadata()\n        external\n        view\n        returns (VaultStorage.WithdrawalQueueMetadata memory);\n\n    function withdrawalRequests(uint256 requestId)\n SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\n/**\n * @title IBridgeAdapter\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Bridge-agnostic adapter interface used by Master / Remote strategies. The adapter\n *         encapsulates one bridge transport (CCIP, CCTP, OP Stack canonical bridge) so the\n *         strategy stays bridge-ignorant. The adapter owns the envelope shape; the strategy\n *         only ever passes its own opaque `payload` bytes.\n *\n *         A single adapter deployment serves all authorised strategies on its chain, with\n *         per-sender lane configuration held inside the adapter. Each adapter is bound to\n *         one peer chain through the lane configuration.\n */\ninterface IBridgeAdapter {\n    /**\n     * @notice Send a message-only payload to the configured peer.\n     * @dev    Strategy passes opaque `payload`; the adapter wraps `(msg.sender, payload)` into\n     *         its transport envelope. For bridges that require a native fee, caller must\n     *         supply `msg.value >= quoteFee.fee` (the adapter checks). No refund of excess \u2014\n     *         overpayment stays on the adapter and can be recovered by governor via\n     *         `transferToken(address(0), ...)`.\n     */\n    function sendMessage(bytes calldata payload) external payable;\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Send a token transfer alongside a message to the configured peer.\n     * @dev    Adapter pulls `amount` of `token` from `msg.sender` via `safeTransferFrom`,\n     *         then forwards via its bridge transport together with the wrapped envelope.\n     *         Same `msg.value` semantics as `sendMessage`.\n     */\n    function sendMessageAndTokens(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes calldata payload\n    ) external payable;\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Quote the fee for the operation described by `(token, amount, payload)`.\n     *\n     * @return fee The fee amount, denominated in `feeToken`. When `feeToken == address(0)`\n     *             this is a native (ETH) fee. When non-zero this is an ERC20 fee in that\n     *             token. When the bridge protocol auto-deducts from the bridged amount\n     *             (e.g., CCTP V2 fast-finality), `fee` is informational only \u2014 the actual\n     *             deduction happens transparently inside the bridge, NOT on the caller.\n     * @return feeToken The token the fee is denominated in. `address(0)` means native.\n     * @return requiresExternalPayment True if the caller must supply `fee` of `feeToken`\n     *             alongside the send call (e.g., via `msg.value` for native). False if the\n     *             bridge protocol handles the fee transparently (e.g., deducts from the\n     *             bridged token amount). The strategy reads this flag to decide whether to\n     *             enforce a `msg.value` check; if false, the caller can ignore `fee`\n     *             entirely.\n     *\n     *         The three-value return separates two orthogonal concerns:\n     *           1. Pre-send caller action (do I need to pay anything separately?)\n     *           2. Post-send accounting (the actual deduction is surfaced via\n     *              `IBridgeReceiver.receiveMessage(... uint256 feePaid)` on the receiving\n     *              side, independent of this quote).\n     */\n    function quoteFee(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes calldata payload\n    )\n        external\n        view\n        returns (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        );\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Per-tx maximum token amount this adapter accepts on outbound, and the implied\n     *         maximum it can deliver on inbound (since outbound and inbound are configured\n     *         as mirror sides of the same protocol lane).\n     *\n     *         Strategies use this in two places:\n     *           - Master.depositAll caps the locally-staged balance by\n     *             `outboundAdapter.maxTransferAmount()` before sending.\n     *           - Master.withdrawAll caps the requested amount by\n     *             `inboundAdapter.maxTransferAmount()`, because Master can't query Remote's\n     *             outbound across chains \u2014 the symmetric inbound adapter holds the same\n     *             protocol-level cap.\n     *\n     *         `0` means \"no enforcement\" (unlimited). Concrete adapters layer additional\n     *         protocol-level constants on top (e.g., CCTPAdapter enforces a hard 10M USDC\n     *         cap regardless of the configured value).\n     */\n    function maxTransferAmount() external view returns (uint256);\n}\n","keccak256":"0xc212efb34e7ad260919d44f2fd6c45507b96a378026e2c29c69e1df5d8423a03","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/interfaces/crosschainV3/IBridgeReceiver.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\n/**\n * @title IBridgeReceiver\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Receiver hook implemented by Master and Remote strategies. The configured inbound\n *         adapter forwards incoming bridge deliveries through this single entry point.\n *\n *         The adapter MUST have transferred any inbound tokens to the strategy before\n *         invoking this function. The `amountReceived` argument carries the actual landed\n *         amount (post any transport-side fee deduction); the strategy accounts on\n *         `amountReceived` and may use `feePaid` for telemetry or sanity checks against the\n *         sender's intended amount carried inside `payload`.\n */\ninterface IBridgeReceiver {\n    /**\n     * @notice Called by the authorised inbound adapter when a message lands.\n     * @param sender         Strategy address on the source chain \u2014 under CREATE3 parity, the\n     *                       same address as the destination strategy on this chain.\n     * @param token          Token delivered alongside the message; `address(0)` for\n     *                       message-only deliveries.\n     * @param amountReceived Actual amount of `token` transferred to this strategy by the\n     *                       adapter immediately before this call (already received).\n     * @param feePaid        Transport-side fee deducted from the sender's intended amount\n     *                       (e.g., CCTP V2 fast-finality fee). Informational; the strategy's\n     *                       accounting is on `amountReceived`.\n     * @param payload        Strategy-owned opaque bytes from the source envelope.\n     */\n    function receiveMessage(\n        address sender,\n        address token,\n        uint256 amountReceived,\n        uint256 feePaid,\n        bytes calldata payload\n    ) external;\n}\n","keccak256":"0x9a33146f1323765e9bf73470a5e1bf3e7e3c34a08f9e360cdd43f8c140cf1979","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/strategies/crosschainV3/AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\nimport { IERC20 } from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/IERC20.sol\";\nimport { SafeERC20 } from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/utils/SafeERC20.sol\";\n\nimport { Governable } from \"../../governance/Governable.sol\";\nimport { IBridgeAdapter } from \"../../interfaces/crosschainV3/IBridgeAdapter.sol\";\nimport { IBridgeReceiver } from \"../../interfaces/crosschainV3/IBridgeReceiver.sol\";\nimport { CrossChainV3Helper } from \"./CrossChainV3Helper.sol\";\n\n/**\n * @title AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Shared base for OUSD V3 Master (L2) and Remote (Ethereum) strategies. Provides:\n *           - Bridge-agnostic outbound / inbound adapter wiring.\n *           - Yield-channel nonce machinery (one yield op in flight at a time).\n *           - Inbound `receiveMessage` entry point with adapter-only access control,\n *             dispatching to a single hook the concrete strategy implements.\n *           - Outbound helpers (`_sendYieldMessage`, `_sendYieldTokensAndMessage`,\n *             `_sendMessage`) that pack `(msgType, nonce, body)` into the strategy-owned\n *             payload, quote the adapter fee, forward exact native via `msg.value`, and\n *             refund any excess back to the caller (user / operator).\n *\n *         The abstract does NOT itself inherit `InitializableAbstractStrategy` \u2014 it stays\n *         small and composable. Concrete Master / Remote contracts mix in\n *         `InitializableAbstractStrategy` separately.\n */\nabstract contract AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy is Governable, IBridgeReceiver {\n    using SafeERC20 for IERC20;\n\n    // --- Events -------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    event OutboundAdapterUpdated(address oldAdapter, address newAdapter);\n    event InboundAdapterUpdated(address oldAdapter, address newAdapter);\n    event OperatorUpdated(address oldOperator, address newOperator);\n    event YieldNonceAdvanced(uint64 nonce);\n    event YieldNonceProcessed(uint64 nonce);\n\n    // --- Storage (all new slots; nothing relocated from any parent) ---------\n\n    /// @notice Adapter used to send outbound messages and tokens to the peer chain.\n    address public outboundAdapter;\n\n    /// @notice Adapter authorised to call `receiveMessage` on this strategy.\n    ///         For atomic bridges the outbound and inbound adapters can be the same address;\n    ///         for split-delivery they're typically different.\n    address public inboundAdapter;\n\n    /// @notice Account allowed to drive periodic, permissioned operations\n    ///         (balance check, settlement, claim trigger). Set by governor.\n    address public operator;\n\n    /// @notice Highest yield-channel nonce ever assigned.\n    uint64 public lastYieldNonce;\n\n    /// @notice Marks each yield-channel nonce as processed (true) once its\n    ///         message round-trip completes.\n    mapping(uint64 => bool) public nonceProcessed;\n\n    /// @notice Timestamp echoed back from the most-recently-accepted balance check ack.\n    ///         Used by `_processBalanceCheckResponse` to enforce strict monotonic ordering\n    ///         when multiple balance checks are in flight at the same yield-nonce window\n    ///         and responses can arrive out of order (CCIP delivery isn't FIFO).\n    // slither-disable-next-line constable-states\n    uint256 public lastBalanceCheckTimestamp;\n\n    /// @dev Reserved for future expansion of this abstract layer.\n    uint256[43] private __gap;\n\n    // --- Modifiers ----------------------------------------------------------\n\n    modifier onlyInboundAdapter() {\n        require(\n            inboundAdapter != address(0) && msg.sender == inboundAdapter,\n            \"V3: only inbound adapter\"\n        );\n        _;\n    }\n\n    // --- Adapter / operator configuration (governor) ------------------------\n\n    function setOutboundAdapter(address _outboundAdapter)\n        external\n        onlyGovernor\n    {\n        _setOutboundAdapter(_outboundAdapter);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Hook for concrete strategies that need to perform token-allowance swaps when\n     *      the outbound adapter changes (e.g., revoke an old adapter's bridgeAsset\n     *      allowance, grant the new one max). Default implementation just rotates the\n     *      stored address; override to add side effects.\n     */\n    function _setOutboundAdapter(address _outboundAdapter) internal virtual {\n        emit OutboundAdapterUpdated(outboundAdapter, _outboundAdapter);\n        outboundAdapter = _outboundAdapter;\n    }\n\n    function setInboundAdapter(address _inboundAdapter) external onlyGovernor {\n        emit InboundAdapterUpdated(inboundAdapter, _inboundAdapter);\n        inboundAdapter = _inboundAdapter;\n    }\n\n    function setOperator(address _operator) external onlyGovernor {\n        emit OperatorUpdated(operator, _operator);\n        operator = _operator;\n    }\n\n    // --- Yield-channel nonce machinery --------------------------------------\n\n    /**\n     * @dev True when a yield-channel operation has been initiated but its ack\n     *      has not yet been processed.\n     */\n    function isYieldOpInFlight() public view returns (bool) {\n        uint64 n = lastYieldNonce;\n        if (n == 0) return false;\n        return !nonceProcessed[n];\n    }\n\n    function _getNextYieldNonce() internal returns (uint64) {\n        require(!isYieldOpInFlight(), \"V3: yield op already in flight\");\n        lastYieldNonce += 1;\n        emit YieldNonceAdvanced(lastYieldNonce);\n        return lastYieldNonce;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Called by the initiating side (typically Master) when an ack lands. Requires the\n     *      nonce to match the most recently issued one and not yet be marked processed.\n     */\n    function _markYieldNonceProcessed(uint64 nonce) internal {\n        require(nonce == lastYieldNonce, \"V3: stale or unknown nonce\");\n        require(!nonceProcessed[nonce], \"V3: nonce already processed\");\n        nonceProcessed[nonce] = true;\n        emit YieldNonceProcessed(nonce);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Called by the receiving side (typically Remote) when an inbound yield-channel\n     *      message arrives. The receiver doesn't issue nonces of its own; it adopts the\n     *      sender's nonce, enforcing strict monotonicity and one-time processing.\n     */\n    function _acceptYieldNonce(uint64 nonce) internal {\n        require(nonce > lastYieldNonce, \"V3: nonce not monotonic\");\n        require(!nonceProcessed[nonce], \"V3: nonce already processed\");\n        lastYieldNonce = nonce;\n        nonceProcessed[nonce] = true;\n        emit YieldNonceProcessed(nonce);\n    }\n\n    // --- Inbound dispatch ---------------------------------------------------\n\n    /**\n     * @inheritdoc IBridgeReceiver\n     * @dev Single ingress for all inbound bridge deliveries. Validates the caller is the\n     *      configured inbound adapter, decodes the strategy-owned `(msgType, nonce, body)`\n     *      from `payload`, and forwards to the concrete strategy's hook. No `nonReentrant`\n     *      here \u2014 the concrete strategy's hook is the right place to apply it.\n     */\n    function receiveMessage(\n        address sender,\n        address token,\n        uint256 amountReceived,\n        uint256 feePaid,\n        bytes calldata payload\n    ) external override onlyInboundAdapter {\n        (uint32 msgType, uint64 nonce, bytes memory body) = CrossChainV3Helper\n            .unpackPayload(payload);\n        _handleBridgeMessage(\n            sender,\n            token,\n            amountReceived,\n            feePaid,\n            msgType,\n            nonce,\n            body\n        );\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Concrete strategies (Master / Remote) override this to dispatch by `msgType` and\n     *      implement the per-message logic. `body` is the message-specific payload (e.g.,\n     *      `abi.encode(newBalance)` for DEPOSIT_ACK).\n     */\n    function _handleBridgeMessage(\n        address sender,\n        address token,\n        uint256 amountReceived,\n        uint256 feePaid,\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal virtual;\n\n    // --- Outbound helpers ---------------------------------------------------\n    //\n    // Two parallel fee paths, distinguished by who pays:\n    //\n    //   _sendUserMessage / _sendUserTokensAndMessage\n    //     User-initiated sends (bridgeOTokenToPeer). msg.value MUST cover the fee.\n    //     No fallback to the strategy's pool. Rationale: an attacker could otherwise drain\n    //     the operator-funded pool by spamming bridge_in/out with msg.value=0. User pays\n    //     for their own bridge tx.\n    //\n    //   _sendOpMessage / _sendOpTokensAndMessage\n    //     Operator/protocol-funded sends (yield channel deposits/withdraws/claims and the\n    //     acks Remote sends in response to inbound). msg.value (if any) lands in\n    //     `address(this).balance` first via `receive()`; we then check `balance >= fee`,\n    //     which covers both pre-funded pool AND any msg.value attached.\n    //\n    // Excess msg.value is NEVER refunded. Overpayment becomes part of the strategy's pool.\n    // Recovery via `transferNative` (governor only). Rationale: refunds add code surface;\n    // callers can quote exactly via `IBridgeAdapter.quoteFee` to avoid leaks.\n\n    /// @dev Operator-funded yield-channel message send (message-only).\n    function _sendYieldMessage(\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        _sendOpMessage(msgType, nonce, body);\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Operator-funded yield-channel send carrying tokens (DEPOSIT, WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK).\n    function _sendYieldTokensAndMessage(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        _sendOpTokensAndMessage(token, amount, msgType, nonce, body);\n    }\n\n    /// @dev User-funded bridge-channel send (BRIDGE_IN / BRIDGE_OUT). msg.value required.\n    function _sendBridgeMessage(\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        _sendUserMessage(msgType, nonce, body);\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Strict user-payment path: msg.value MUST cover fee. Pool is NOT consulted \u2014\n    ///      even if it has funds, a short user payment reverts. This is the security gate\n    ///      that prevents bridge_in/out from being a pool-drain vector.\n    function _sendUserMessage(\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        bytes memory payload = CrossChainV3Helper.packPayload(\n            msgType,\n            nonce,\n            body\n        );\n        address adapter = outboundAdapter;\n        (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        ) = IBridgeAdapter(adapter).quoteFee(address(0), 0, payload);\n        if (requiresExternalPayment) {\n            // Only native fee supported today. ERC20 fee tokens (e.g., LINK-mode CCIP)\n            // would need explicit allowance handling; not implemented here. Forces any\n            // future fee-token addition to be an explicit override.\n            require(feeToken == address(0), \"V3: only native fee supported\");\n            require(msg.value >= fee, \"V3: insufficient user fee\");\n            // slither-disable-next-line arbitrary-send-eth\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessage{ value: fee }(payload);\n        } else {\n            // CCTP-style: protocol auto-deducts from bridged amount; no caller action.\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessage(payload);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Pool-funded path: native fee paid from `address(this).balance`. msg.value (if\n    ///      any) already lands in balance via `receive()`, so this naturally covers both\n    ///      pre-funded pool AND inline operator top-ups.\n    function _sendOpMessage(\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        bytes memory payload = CrossChainV3Helper.packPayload(\n            msgType,\n            nonce,\n            body\n        );\n        address adapter = outboundAdapter;\n        (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        ) = IBridgeAdapter(adapter).quoteFee(address(0), 0, payload);\n        if (requiresExternalPayment) {\n            require(feeToken == address(0), \"V3: only native fee supported\");\n            require(address(this).balance >= fee, \"V3: pool unfunded\");\n            // slither-disable-next-line arbitrary-send-eth\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessage{ value: fee }(payload);\n        } else {\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessage(payload);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Token-carrying variant of `_sendUserMessage`. Unused for V3 today (bridge\n    ///      channel is message-only on the wire), but kept symmetric for future use.\n    function _sendUserTokensAndMessage(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        bytes memory payload = CrossChainV3Helper.packPayload(\n            msgType,\n            nonce,\n            body\n        );\n        address adapter = outboundAdapter;\n        (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        ) = IBridgeAdapter(adapter).quoteFee(token, amount, payload);\n        if (requiresExternalPayment) {\n            require(feeToken == address(0), \"V3: only native fee supported\");\n            require(msg.value >= fee, \"V3: insufficient user fee\");\n            // slither-disable-next-line arbitrary-send-eth\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessageAndTokens{ value: fee }(\n                token,\n                amount,\n                payload\n            );\n        } else {\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessageAndTokens(\n                token,\n                amount,\n                payload\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Token-carrying variant of `_sendOpMessage`. Used by DEPOSIT (Master) and\n    ///      WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK with tokens (Remote).\n    function _sendOpTokensAndMessage(\n        address token,\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        bytes memory payload = CrossChainV3Helper.packPayload(\n            msgType,\n            nonce,\n            body\n        );\n        address adapter = outboundAdapter;\n        (\n            uint256 fee,\n            address feeToken,\n            bool requiresExternalPayment\n        ) = IBridgeAdapter(adapter).quoteFee(token, amount, payload);\n        if (requiresExternalPayment) {\n            require(feeToken == address(0), \"V3: only native fee supported\");\n            require(address(this).balance >= fee, \"V3: pool unfunded\");\n            // slither-disable-next-line arbitrary-send-eth\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessageAndTokens{ value: fee }(\n                token,\n                amount,\n                payload\n            );\n        } else {\n            IBridgeAdapter(adapter).sendMessageAndTokens(\n                token,\n                amount,\n                payload\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Sweep native ETH out of the strategy to governor. Used to drain the fee\n    ///         pool (operator rotation, decommission) or recover stray donations (a user\n    ///         that overpaid msg.value when calling `bridgeOTokenToPeer`).\n    function transferNative(uint256 amount) external onlyGovernor {\n        // slither-disable-next-line low-level-calls\n        (bool ok, ) = governor().call{ value: amount }(\"\");\n        require(ok, \"V3: native sweep failed\");\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Strategy accepts native ETH unconditionally. Lands in `address(this).balance`\n    ///      and serves as the fee pool. NEVER counted toward `checkBalance` \u2014 that function\n    ///      only sums bridge-asset-denominated slots, so ETH on this contract is naturally\n    ///      invisible to the L2 vault's accounting. (No explicit \"exclude ETH\" code needed.)\n    receive() external payable virtual {}\n}\n","keccak256":"0xc38346304bb6535cf44652314e570d138b1a499619ed5e77cf1f249c00c1175a","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/strategies/crosschainV3/AbstractWOTokenStrategy.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\nimport { IERC20, SafeERC20, InitializableAbstractStrategy } from \"../../utils/InitializableAbstractStrategy.sol\";\nimport { IVault } from \"../../interfaces/IVault.sol\";\n\nimport { AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy } from \"./AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy.sol\";\nimport { CrossChainV3Helper } from \"./CrossChainV3Helper.sol\";\n\n/**\n * @title AbstractWOTokenStrategy\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Shared base for the wOToken cross-chain strategy pair (Master on L2, Remote on\n *         Ethereum). Lifts everything that's duplicated between Master and Remote:\n *\n *           - Constants + immutables (bridgeAsset, oToken, MAX_BRIDGE_CALL_GAS).\n *           - Bridge-channel state (bridgeAdjustment, consumedBridgeIds, bridgeIdCounter).\n *           - Generic bridge-channel mechanics: outbound send (`bridgeOTokenToPeer`),\n *             inbound dispatch (`_handleInboundBridgeMessage`), replay protection, signed\n *             `bridgeAdjustment` bookkeeping, optional post-delivery callback.\n *           - `_abstractSetPToken` and `collectRewardTokens` no-op stubs (Strategy base\n *             requires them; neither strategy uses them).\n *           - `onlyOperatorGovernorOrStrategist` modifier (operator OR strategist OR governor).\n *\n *         Concrete strategies implement four hooks for the small middle of each bridge op\n *         that differs between the two sides:\n *\n *           - `_bridgeOutboundMsgType()` \u2014 Master: BRIDGE_OUT, Remote: BRIDGE_IN.\n *           - `_preflightBridgeOutbound(amount)` \u2014 Master: liquidity check, Remote: no-op.\n *           - `_consumeOTokenForBridge(amount)` \u2014 Master: burn via vault, Remote: wrap to wOToken.\n *           - `_deliverOTokenForBridge(amount, recipient)` \u2014 Master: mint+transfer, Remote: unwrap+transfer.\n */\nabstract contract AbstractWOTokenStrategy is\n    AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy,\n    InitializableAbstractStrategy\n{\n    using SafeERC20 for IERC20;\n\n    // --- Constants & immutables --------------------------------------------\n\n    /// @notice Maximum gas forwarded to the optional post-delivery `callData` call on\n    ///         the bridge channel. Caps griefing surface; users can request lower per call.\n    uint32 public constant MAX_BRIDGE_CALL_GAS = 500000;\n\n    /// @notice Maximum protocol fee on the bridge channel (10% in basis points).\n    uint256 public constant MAX_BRIDGE_FEE_BPS = 1000;\n\n    /// @notice Asset that bridges between Master and Remote (USDC for OUSD V3, WETH for OETHb).\n    address public immutable bridgeAsset;\n\n    /// @notice OToken on this chain (the rebasing OToken \u2014 OUSD, OETH, OETHb, etc.).\n    address public immutable oToken;\n\n    // --- Storage (all new slots) -------------------------------------------\n\n    /// @notice Signed net delta from bridge-channel activity since the last settlement.\n    ///         BRIDGE_IN (mint locally / wrap locally) \u2192 increases.\n    ///         BRIDGE_OUT (burn locally / unwrap locally) \u2192 decreases.\n    int256 public bridgeAdjustment;\n\n    /// @notice Replay protection for the nonceless bridge channel.\n    mapping(bytes32 => bool) public consumedBridgeIds;\n\n    /// @notice Monotonic counter used to generate fresh bridgeIds for outbound BRIDGE_IN\n    ///         / BRIDGE_OUT operations. Combined with `address(this)` for global uniqueness.\n    uint256 public bridgeIdCounter;\n\n    /// @notice Protocol fee on the bridge channel in basis points (1 bp = 0.01%). Default\n    ///         0; capped at `MAX_BRIDGE_FEE_BPS`. When > 0, the source side consumes the\n    ///         full `_amount` of OToken while the envelope carries `net = _amount - fee`,\n    ///         so the peer only delivers `net`. The retained `fee` worth of backing flows\n    ///         through the next `BALANCE_CHECK` and lifts the vault's rebase by the\n    ///         fee.\n    uint256 public bridgeFeeBps;\n\n    /// @dev Reserved for future expansion of this abstract layer.\n    uint256[43] private __gap;\n\n    // --- Events -------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    event BridgeRequested(\n        bytes32 indexed bridgeId,\n        address indexed sender,\n        address indexed recipient,\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint256 fee,\n        bytes callData,\n        uint32 callGasLimit\n    );\n    event BridgeDelivered(\n        bytes32 indexed bridgeId,\n        address indexed recipient,\n        uint256 amount\n    );\n    event BridgeFeeBpsUpdated(uint256 oldBps, uint256 newBps);\n    event BridgeCallSucceeded(\n        bytes32 indexed bridgeId,\n        address indexed recipient,\n        uint256 amount\n    );\n    event BridgeCallFailed(\n        bytes32 indexed bridgeId,\n        address indexed recipient,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes returnData\n    );\n\n    // --- Construction -------------------------------------------------------\n\n    constructor(\n        BaseStrategyConfig memory _stratConfig,\n        address _bridgeAsset,\n        address _oToken\n    ) InitializableAbstractStrategy(_stratConfig) {\n        require(_bridgeAsset != address(0), \"WOT: bridge asset required\");\n        require(_oToken != address(0), \"WOT: oToken required\");\n        bridgeAsset = _bridgeAsset;\n        oToken = _oToken;\n    }\n\n    // --- Modifiers ----------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /// @notice Permits the operator, strategist, or governor.\n    modifier onlyOperatorGovernorOrStrategist() {\n        require(\n            msg.sender == operator ||\n                isGovernor() ||\n                msg.sender == IVault(vaultAddress).strategistAddr(),\n            \"WOT: not authorised\"\n        );\n        _;\n    }\n\n    // --- Strategy-base shims (no-op) ---------------------------------------\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function supportsAsset(address _asset) public view override returns (bool) {\n        return _asset == bridgeAsset;\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function _abstractSetPToken(address, address) internal override {}\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function collectRewardTokens()\n        external\n        override\n        onlyHarvesterOrStrategist\n        nonReentrant\n    {}\n\n    // --- Bridge channel: outbound -------------------------------------------\n\n    /**\n     * @notice User-initiated bridge: burn (Master) or wrap (Remote) `_amount` of OToken\n     *         locally and instruct the peer chain to deliver the equivalent amount.\n     * @param _amount        OToken amount to bridge.\n     * @param _recipient     Destination on the peer chain. `address(0)` defaults to msg.sender.\n     * @param _callData      Optional calldata invoked on `_recipient` after token delivery on\n     *                       the destination side. Empty for plain bridge.\n     * @param _callGasLimit  Per-call gas cap; must be \u2264 MAX_BRIDGE_CALL_GAS.\n     */\n    function bridgeOTokenToPeer(\n        uint256 _amount,\n        address _recipient,\n        bytes calldata _callData,\n        uint32 _callGasLimit\n    ) external payable nonReentrant {\n        require(_amount > 0, \"WOT: zero bridge\");\n        require(outboundAdapter != address(0), \"WOT: outbound not set\");\n        require(\n            _callGasLimit <= MAX_BRIDGE_CALL_GAS,\n            \"WOT: callGasLimit too high\"\n        );\n        require(\n            _callData.length == 0 || _callGasLimit > 0,\n            \"WOT: callData needs gas\"\n        );\n        _bridgeOutboundExec(_amount, _recipient, _callData, _callGasLimit);\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Split from `bridgeOTokenToPeer` to keep the public function's stack within\n    ///      limits \u2014 the burn-full/deliver-net + envelope build chain pushes several\n    ///      locals that crowd the verifier.\n    function _bridgeOutboundExec(\n        uint256 _amount,\n        address _recipient,\n        bytes calldata _callData,\n        uint32 _callGasLimit\n    ) private {\n        // Burn-full / deliver-net: protocol fee is consumed on the source (full `_amount`)\n        // but only `net` flows across the bridge; the difference is the retained backing\n        // that lifts the next rebase.\n        uint256 fee = (_amount * bridgeFeeBps) / 10000;\n        uint256 net = _amount - fee;\n        require(net > 0, \"WOT: net zero after fee\");\n\n        // Pre-flight against `net` (what the peer must produce).\n        _preflightBridgeOutbound(net);\n\n        address recipient = _recipient == address(0) ? msg.sender : _recipient;\n\n        // Master burns FULL `_amount`; Remote wraps FULL `_amount`. The fee portion stays\n        // as backing on the wOToken side and accrues to yield via the next BALANCE_CHECK.\n        _consumeOTokenForBridge(_amount);\n\n        uint32 msgType = _bridgeOutboundMsgType();\n        // Accounting captures the obligation that's actually leaving \u2014 `net`.\n        _applyBridgeAdjustment(msgType, net);\n\n        bytes32 bridgeId = _nextBridgeId();\n        bytes memory body = CrossChainV3Helper.encodeBridgeUserPayload(\n            CrossChainV3Helper.BridgeUserPayload({\n                bridgeId: bridgeId,\n                amount: net,\n                recipient: recipient,\n                callData: _callData,\n                callGasLimit: _callGasLimit\n            })\n        );\n        _sendBridgeMessage(msgType, 0, body);\n\n        emit BridgeRequested(\n            bridgeId,\n            msg.sender,\n            recipient,\n            net,\n            fee,\n            _callData,\n            _callGasLimit\n        );\n    }\n\n    // --- Governance --------------------------------------------------------\n\n    function setBridgeFeeBps(uint256 _bps) external onlyGovernor {\n        require(_bps <= MAX_BRIDGE_FEE_BPS, \"WOT: fee too high\");\n        emit BridgeFeeBpsUpdated(bridgeFeeBps, _bps);\n        bridgeFeeBps = _bps;\n    }\n\n    // --- Bridge channel: inbound -------------------------------------------\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Called by concrete strategies from `_handleBridgeMessage` when an inbound\n     *      BRIDGE_IN / BRIDGE_OUT envelope arrives. Replay-checked, applies signed\n     *      `bridgeAdjustment`, invokes the side-specific delivery hook, runs the optional\n     *      post-delivery callback.\n     */\n    function _handleInboundBridgeMessage(\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint256 amount,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal {\n        CrossChainV3Helper.BridgeUserPayload memory p = CrossChainV3Helper\n            .decodeBridgeUserPayload(body);\n\n        require(!consumedBridgeIds[p.bridgeId], \"WOT: bridgeId replayed\");\n        // Bridge-channel messages are message-only by design; tokens never ride along.\n        require(amount == 0, \"WOT: bridge-in tokens not expected\");\n        require(\n            p.callGasLimit <= MAX_BRIDGE_CALL_GAS,\n            \"WOT: callGasLimit too high\"\n        );\n\n        // CEI: mark consumed, update accounting, deliver tokens, optional call.\n        consumedBridgeIds[p.bridgeId] = true;\n        _applyBridgeAdjustment(msgType, p.amount);\n\n        // Side-specific delivery (Master: mint + transfer; Remote: unwrap + transfer).\n        _deliverOTokenForBridge(p.amount, p.recipient);\n\n        emit BridgeDelivered(p.bridgeId, p.recipient, p.amount);\n\n        if (p.callData.length == 0) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        _postDeliveryCall(p);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Best-effort post-delivery call on the recipient. Never reverts; tokens have\n     *      already been delivered before this runs. No msg.value forwarded; gas bounded\n     *      by `p.callGasLimit` (already capped above by MAX_BRIDGE_CALL_GAS).\n     */\n    function _postDeliveryCall(CrossChainV3Helper.BridgeUserPayload memory p)\n        private\n    {\n        // slither-disable-next-line low-level-calls,unchecked-lowlevel\n        (bool ok, bytes memory ret) = p.recipient.call{\n            value: 0,\n            gas: p.callGasLimit\n        }(p.callData);\n        if (ok) {\n            emit BridgeCallSucceeded(p.bridgeId, p.recipient, p.amount);\n        } else {\n            emit BridgeCallFailed(p.bridgeId, p.recipient, p.amount, ret);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Apply the signed delta to `bridgeAdjustment` based on the message type. Both\n     *      Master and Remote use the same convention: BRIDGE_IN increases (mint/wrap),\n     *      BRIDGE_OUT decreases (burn/unwrap). The sign is determined by the message type\n     *      alone \u2014 no per-side configuration needed.\n     */\n    function _applyBridgeAdjustment(uint32 msgType, uint256 amount) internal {\n        if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.BRIDGE_IN) {\n            bridgeAdjustment += int256(amount);\n        } else {\n            // BRIDGE_OUT (only other valid bridge-channel type; caller enforces this).\n            bridgeAdjustment -= int256(amount);\n        }\n    }\n\n    function _nextBridgeId() internal returns (bytes32) {\n        bridgeIdCounter += 1;\n        return keccak256(abi.encode(address(this), bridgeIdCounter));\n    }\n\n    // --- Hooks (concrete strategies implement) -----------------------------\n\n    /// @notice Outbound bridge-channel message type. Master: BRIDGE_OUT, Remote: BRIDGE_IN.\n    function _bridgeOutboundMsgType() internal pure virtual returns (uint32);\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Side-specific pre-flight check before consuming OToken on outbound bridge.\n     *         Master: ensures Remote has reported (or expects via bridgeAdjustment) enough\n     *         OToken to cover the delivery. Remote: no-op (wrapping always succeeds when\n     *         the user supplies the OToken).\n     */\n    function _preflightBridgeOutbound(uint256 amount) internal view virtual;\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Pull OToken from `msg.sender` and consume it on this chain.\n     *         Master: burn via the OToken vault. Remote: wrap to wOToken via the ERC-4626.\n     */\n    function _consumeOTokenForBridge(uint256 amount) internal virtual;\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Produce OToken on this chain and deliver it to `recipient`.\n     *         Master: mint via the OToken vault, then transfer. Remote: unwrap wOToken to\n     *         OToken, then transfer.\n     */\n    function _deliverOTokenForBridge(uint256 amount, address recipient)\n        internal\n        virtual;\n}\n","keccak256":"0x33f242be6183f4aa3572888eb52b35cffc3811b8b34a48e6e21023aa1635ad50","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/strategies/crosschainV3/CrossChainV3Helper.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\n/**\n * @title CrossChainV3Helper\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @dev Strategy-level message-type constants and payload codecs for OUSD V3\n *      cross-chain messages. The wire envelope (sender + intendedAmount + payload) is\n *      bridge-adapter-internal; strategies only encode and decode the per-message-type\n *      payloads below, with the message type discriminator embedded inside the payload\n *      itself.\n */\nlibrary CrossChainV3Helper {\n    // --- Message type discriminators ---------------------------------------\n\n    // Yield channel (nonce-gated, one operation in flight at a time)\n\n    /// @notice Master \u2192 Remote: deposit `amount` of bridgeAsset (carried by the adapter).\n    uint32 internal constant DEPOSIT = 1;\n    /// @notice Remote \u2192 Master: deposit acknowledgement with Remote's new checkBalance.\n    uint32 internal constant DEPOSIT_ACK = 2;\n    /// @notice Master \u2192 Remote: leg-1 withdrawal request for `amount` of bridgeAsset.\n    uint32 internal constant WITHDRAW_REQUEST = 3;\n    /// @notice Remote \u2192 Master: leg-1 acknowledgement with Remote's new checkBalance.\n    uint32 internal constant WITHDRAW_REQUEST_ACK = 4;\n    /// @notice Master \u2192 Remote: leg-2 trigger to ship the previously-queued amount.\n    uint32 internal constant WITHDRAW_CLAIM = 5;\n    /// @notice Remote \u2192 Master: leg-2 ack carrying bridgeAsset on success.\n    uint32 internal constant WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK = 6;\n    /// @notice Master \u2192 Remote: read Remote's balance snapshot at a given timestamp.\n    uint32 internal constant BALANCE_CHECK_REQUEST = 7;\n    /// @notice Remote \u2192 Master: balance response (balance + originating timestamp).\n    uint32 internal constant BALANCE_CHECK_RESPONSE = 8;\n    /// @notice Master \u2192 Remote: clear the bridge-adjustment accounting on both sides.\n    uint32 internal constant SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING = 9;\n    /// @notice Remote \u2192 Master: settlement acknowledgement with Remote's new checkBalance.\n    uint32 internal constant SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING_ACK = 10;\n\n    // Bridge channel (nonceless, multiple operations in flight)\n\n    /// @notice Remote \u2192 Master: user-driven bridge of OToken from Ethereum onto the L2.\n    uint32 internal constant BRIDGE_IN = 11;\n    /// @notice Master \u2192 Remote: user-driven bridge of OToken from L2 back to Ethereum.\n    uint32 internal constant BRIDGE_OUT = 12;\n\n    // --- Bridge user payload (BRIDGE_IN / BRIDGE_OUT) -----------------------\n\n    /**\n     * @dev User-supplied payload for the bridge channel. Encoded inside the\n     *      envelope body. The destination strategy uses `bridgeId` for replay\n     *      protection (see Master / Remote `consumedBridgeIds` mapping) and\n     *      validates `callGasLimit` against its adapter-configured maximum\n     *      before issuing the optional post-delivery call.\n     */\n    struct BridgeUserPayload {\n        bytes32 bridgeId;\n        uint256 amount;\n        address recipient;\n        bytes callData;\n        uint32 callGasLimit;\n    }\n\n    // --- Strategy-level envelope (msgType + nonce + body) -------------------\n    //\n    // Strategies wrap their per-op body bytes inside a small strategy-owned envelope so a\n    // single `payload` field can carry message-type discrimination and a yield-channel\n    // nonce without leaking those concerns into the bridge adapter. The adapter sees the\n    // strategy envelope as opaque bytes.\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Build the strategy-level envelope: `abi.encode(msgType, nonce, body)`.\n     */\n    function packPayload(\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal pure returns (bytes memory) {\n        return abi.encode(msgType, nonce, body);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Decode the strategy-level envelope.\n     */\n    function unpackPayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (\n            uint32 msgType,\n            uint64 nonce,\n            bytes memory body\n        )\n    {\n        (msgType, nonce, body) = abi.decode(payload, (uint32, uint64, bytes));\n    }\n\n    // --- Per-message payload encoders / decoders ----------------------------\n    //\n    // DEPOSIT                       : payload empty; amount is carried by the adapter\n    // DEPOSIT_ACK                   : payload = abi.encode(newBalance)\n    // WITHDRAW_REQUEST              : payload = abi.encode(amount)\n    // WITHDRAW_REQUEST_ACK          : payload = abi.encode(newBalance)\n    // WITHDRAW_CLAIM                : payload empty\n    // WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK            : payload = abi.encode(newBalance, success, amount)\n    // BALANCE_CHECK_REQUEST         : payload = abi.encode(timestamp)\n    // BALANCE_CHECK_RESPONSE        : payload = abi.encode(balance, timestamp)\n    // SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING      : payload empty\n    // SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING_ACK  : payload = abi.encode(newBalance)\n    // BRIDGE_IN / BRIDGE_OUT        : payload = abi.encode(BridgeUserPayload)\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Encode the single-uint256 payload used by DEPOSIT_ACK,\n     *         WITHDRAW_REQUEST_ACK, and SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING_ACK.\n     * @param newBalance Remote's `checkBalance(bridgeAsset)` snapshot after the op.\n     */\n    function encodeNewBalancePayload(uint256 newBalance)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (bytes memory)\n    {\n        return abi.encode(newBalance);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Decode the single-uint256 payload above.\n    function decodeNewBalancePayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (uint256 newBalance)\n    {\n        return abi.decode(payload, (uint256));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Encode the WITHDRAW_REQUEST payload (the leg-1 amount Master wants).\n     * @param amount  bridgeAsset units to withdraw.\n     */\n    function encodeAmountPayload(uint256 amount)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (bytes memory)\n    {\n        return abi.encode(amount);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Decode the WITHDRAW_REQUEST payload.\n    function decodeAmountPayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (uint256 amount)\n    {\n        return abi.decode(payload, (uint256));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Encode the WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK payload. The only R\u2192M yield message that\n     *         carries tokens \u2014 `amount` pins the exact bridgeAsset bundled with the\n     *         message (0 on NACK or message-only) so split-delivery receivers can set\n     *         `expectedAmount` without inspecting the bridge transport.\n     * @param newBalance Remote's `checkBalance` after the claim leg.\n     * @param success    `true` if the claim shipped tokens, `false` if leg-2 NACK'd.\n     * @param amount     bridgeAsset units bundled with this ack; 0 when `success` is false.\n     */\n    function encodeWithdrawClaimAckPayload(\n        uint256 newBalance,\n        bool success,\n        uint256 amount\n    ) internal pure returns (bytes memory) {\n        return abi.encode(newBalance, success, amount);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Decode the WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK 3-tuple payload.\n    function decodeWithdrawClaimAckPayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (\n            uint256 newBalance,\n            bool success,\n            uint256 amount\n        )\n    {\n        return abi.decode(payload, (uint256, bool, uint256));\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Encode the BALANCE_CHECK_REQUEST payload (origin timestamp).\n    function encodeBalanceCheckRequestPayload(uint256 timestamp)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (bytes memory)\n    {\n        return abi.encode(timestamp);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Decode the BALANCE_CHECK_REQUEST payload.\n    function decodeBalanceCheckRequestPayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (uint256 timestamp)\n    {\n        return abi.decode(payload, (uint256));\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Encode the BALANCE_CHECK_RESPONSE payload (balance + originating ts).\n    function encodeBalanceCheckResponsePayload(\n        uint256 balance,\n        uint256 timestamp\n    ) internal pure returns (bytes memory) {\n        return abi.encode(balance, timestamp);\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Decode the BALANCE_CHECK_RESPONSE 2-tuple payload.\n    function decodeBalanceCheckResponsePayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (uint256 balance, uint256 timestamp)\n    {\n        return abi.decode(payload, (uint256, uint256));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Encode the BRIDGE_IN / BRIDGE_OUT payload \u2014 packs the 5 user-supplied\n     *         fields the receiving strategy needs to deliver tokens and run the\n     *         optional post-delivery call.\n     */\n    function encodeBridgeUserPayload(BridgeUserPayload memory p)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (bytes memory)\n    {\n        return\n            abi.encode(\n                p.bridgeId,\n                p.amount,\n                p.recipient,\n                p.callData,\n                p.callGasLimit\n            );\n    }\n\n    /// @notice Decode the BRIDGE_IN / BRIDGE_OUT payload into a `BridgeUserPayload`.\n    function decodeBridgeUserPayload(bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n        pure\n        returns (BridgeUserPayload memory)\n    {\n        (\n            bytes32 bridgeId,\n            uint256 amount,\n            address recipient,\n            bytes memory callData,\n            uint32 callGasLimit\n        ) = abi.decode(payload, (bytes32, uint256, address, bytes, uint32));\n        return\n            BridgeUserPayload({\n                bridgeId: bridgeId,\n                amount: amount,\n                recipient: recipient,\n                callData: callData,\n                callGasLimit: callGasLimit\n            });\n    }\n}\n","keccak256":"0x22ea4faf6d8b2ac9fb98665db7a03aed732282573bf3df0a876bf1eff2a20e02","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/strategies/crosschainV3/RemoteWOTokenStrategy.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\nimport { IERC20, SafeERC20, InitializableAbstractStrategy } from \"../../utils/InitializableAbstractStrategy.sol\";\nimport { IERC4626 } from \"../../../lib/openzeppelin/interfaces/IERC4626.sol\";\nimport { IVault } from \"../../interfaces/IVault.sol\";\n\n// solhint-disable-next-line no-unused-import\nimport { AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy } from \"./AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy.sol\";\nimport { AbstractWOTokenStrategy } from \"./AbstractWOTokenStrategy.sol\";\nimport { CrossChainV3Helper } from \"./CrossChainV3Helper.sol\";\n\n/**\n * @title RemoteWOTokenStrategy\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n *\n * @notice Yield-side leg of the wOToken cross-chain strategy pair. Holds wOToken shares\n *         on behalf of the peer Master. Runs the 2-step pipeline:\n *\n *           inbound : bridgeAsset \u2192 OToken (via OToken vault `mint`) \u2192 wOToken (via 4626.deposit)\n *           outbound: wOToken (via 4626.withdraw) \u2192 OToken \u2192 bridgeAsset (via OToken vault redeem)\n *\n *         Remote is NOT registered with any vault \u2014 it's a custodian for shares held on\n *         behalf of the peer Master. The `oTokenVault` parameter points at the local\n *         OToken vault on this chain (e.g. the OUSD vault on Ethereum or the OETH vault\n *         on Ethereum).\n *\n *         For the full Remote state-transition table (Idle \u2192 Requested \u2192 Claimed \u2192 Bridging-out\n *         \u2192 Completed) see the V3 implementation plan.\n */\ncontract RemoteWOTokenStrategy is AbstractWOTokenStrategy {\n    using SafeERC20 for IERC20;\n\n    // --- Immutables --------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /// @notice ERC-4626 wrapper of the OToken (wOUSD or wOETH).\n    address public immutable woToken;\n\n    /// @notice Yield-side OToken vault. Used to convert bridgeAsset \u2194 OToken via mint / redeem.\n    address public immutable oTokenVault;\n\n    // --- Storage (all new slots; nothing from any parent is relocated) -----\n\n    /// @notice OToken-vault queue handle. 0 = no outstanding queue request.\n    uint256 public outstandingRequestId;\n\n    /// @notice BridgeAsset value sitting in the OToken vault queue, not yet claimed.\n    ///         Set when `requestWithdrawal` runs, cleared when `claimWithdrawal` succeeds.\n    uint256 public queuedAmount;\n\n    /// @notice Originally-requested bridgeAsset amount for the outstanding withdrawal.\n    ///         Set in `_processWithdrawRequest`, refined to the actually-claimed amount\n    ///         once `_opportunisticClaim` succeeds, cleared on successful leg-2 delivery.\n    ///         Caps the value leg-2 may ship to Master, defeating residual/donation over-send.\n    uint256 public outstandingRequestAmount;\n\n    /// @dev Reserved for future expansion.\n    uint256[42] private __gap;\n\n    // --- Events -------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    event YieldDepositProcessed(\n        uint64 nonce,\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint256 newBalance\n    );\n    event WithdrawRequestProcessed(\n        uint64 nonce,\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint256 requestId\n    );\n    event WithdrawClaimDelivered(\n        uint64 nonce,\n        uint256 amount,\n        uint256 newBalance\n    );\n    event WithdrawClaimNack(uint64 nonce, uint256 newBalance);\n    event RemoteWithdrawalClaimed(uint256 requestId, uint256 amount);\n\n    // --- Construction / initialisation -------------------------------------\n\n    constructor(\n        BaseStrategyConfig memory _stratConfig,\n        address _bridgeAsset,\n        address _oToken,\n        address _woToken,\n        address _oTokenVault\n    ) AbstractWOTokenStrategy(_stratConfig, _bridgeAsset, _oToken) {\n        // Remote has no vault and uses `woToken` as its \"platform\" for the strategy registry.\n        require(\n            _stratConfig.vaultAddress == address(0),\n            \"Remote: vault must be zero\"\n        );\n        require(_woToken != address(0), \"Remote: woToken required\");\n        require(_oTokenVault != address(0), \"Remote: oTokenVault required\");\n        require(\n            _stratConfig.platformAddress == _woToken,\n            \"Remote: platform must be woToken\"\n        );\n        woToken = _woToken;\n        oTokenVault = _oTokenVault;\n    }\n\n    function initialize(address _operator) external onlyGovernor initializer {\n        operator = _operator;\n\n        address[] memory rewardTokens = new address[](0);\n        address[] memory assets = new address[](1);\n        address[] memory pTokens = new address[](1);\n        assets[0] = bridgeAsset;\n        pTokens[0] = woToken;\n\n        InitializableAbstractStrategy._initialize(\n            rewardTokens,\n            assets,\n            pTokens\n        );\n    }\n\n    // --- Required strategy overrides ---------------------------------------\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function checkBalance(address _asset)\n        external\n        view\n        override\n        returns (uint256)\n    {\n        require(_asset == bridgeAsset, \"Remote: unsupported asset\");\n        return _viewCheckBalance();\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function safeApproveAllTokens()\n        external\n        override\n        onlyGovernor\n        nonReentrant\n    {\n        // Static (token, spender) pairs the strategy ever transfers through:\n        //   bridgeAsset \u2192 oTokenVault   (vault.mint pulls WETH on deposit)\n        //   oToken      \u2192 oTokenVault   (vault.requestWithdrawal pulls OToken on withdraw)\n        //   oToken      \u2192 woToken       (ERC-4626 deposit / withdraw of OToken shares)\n        // One-shot: approves to type(uint256).max so the per-op approval dance isn't needed.\n        // The dynamic (bridgeAsset \u2192 outboundAdapter) pair is managed by `setOutboundAdapter`.\n        IERC20(bridgeAsset).safeApprove(oTokenVault, type(uint256).max);\n        IERC20(oToken).safeApprove(oTokenVault, type(uint256).max);\n        IERC20(oToken).safeApprove(woToken, type(uint256).max);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @inheritdoc AbstractCrossChainV3Strategy\n     * @dev Rotates the bridgeAsset allowance from the old adapter to the new one so leg-2\n     *      ship doesn't need a per-call approve.\n     */\n    function _setOutboundAdapter(address _outboundAdapter)\n        internal\n        virtual\n        override\n    {\n        address old = outboundAdapter;\n        if (old != address(0) && old != _outboundAdapter) {\n            IERC20(bridgeAsset).safeApprove(old, 0);\n        }\n        // slither-disable-next-line reentrancy-no-eth\n        super._setOutboundAdapter(_outboundAdapter);\n        if (_outboundAdapter != address(0) && old != _outboundAdapter) {\n            IERC20(bridgeAsset).safeApprove(\n                _outboundAdapter,\n                type(uint256).max\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function deposit(address, uint256)\n        external\n        view\n        override\n        onlyVaultOrGovernor\n    {\n        revert(\"Remote: use bridge\");\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function depositAll() external view override onlyVaultOrGovernor {\n        revert(\"Remote: use bridge\");\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function withdraw(\n        address,\n        address,\n        uint256\n    ) external view override onlyVaultOrGovernor {\n        revert(\"Remote: use bridge\");\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc InitializableAbstractStrategy\n    function withdrawAll() external view override onlyVaultOrGovernor {\n        revert(\"Remote: use bridge\");\n    }\n\n    // --- Inbound dispatch --------------------------------------------------\n\n    function _handleBridgeMessage(\n        address, // sender\n        address, // token\n        uint256 amountReceived,\n        uint256, // feePaid\n        uint32 msgType,\n        uint64 nonce,\n        bytes memory body\n    ) internal override {\n        if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.DEPOSIT) {\n            _processYieldDeposit(nonce, amountReceived);\n        } else if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.WITHDRAW_REQUEST) {\n            _processWithdrawRequest(nonce, body);\n        } else if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.WITHDRAW_CLAIM) {\n            _processWithdrawClaim(nonce);\n        } else if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.BRIDGE_OUT) {\n            _handleInboundBridgeMessage(msgType, amountReceived, body);\n        } else if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.BALANCE_CHECK_REQUEST) {\n            _processBalanceCheckRequest(nonce, body);\n        } else if (msgType == CrossChainV3Helper.SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING) {\n            _processSettlement(nonce, body);\n        } else {\n            revert(\"Remote: unsupported message type\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Reports the YIELD-ONLY baseline: `_viewCheckBalance() - bridgeAdjustment`.\n    ///      This cancels bridge-channel deltas on both sides \u2014 for each BRIDGE_OUT,\n    ///      `_viewCheckBalance` drops by `net` AND `bridgeAdjustment` drops by `net`, so\n    ///      the difference stays constant. Bridge channel becomes invisible at this layer.\n    ///\n    ///      Master combines this yield-only value with its own `bridgeAdjustment` to\n    ///      reconstruct the true backing total via `checkBalance`. The math is consistent\n    ///      regardless of whether bridge messages have been processed on Remote yet \u2014\n    ///      see the design doc for the full case analysis.\n    ///\n    ///      DOES NOT call `_acceptYieldNonce`: balance check is non-blocking, read-only,\n    ///      and the nonce is echoed back unchanged so Master can validate it's still in\n    ///      the same yield epoch.\n    function _processBalanceCheckRequest(uint64 nonce, bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n    {\n        uint256 srcTimestamp = CrossChainV3Helper\n            .decodeBalanceCheckRequestPayload(payload);\n        int256 yieldOnly = int256(_viewCheckBalance()) - bridgeAdjustment;\n        // Defensive: yield-only baseline should never go negative in healthy operation.\n        // Each BRIDGE_IN increases `_viewCheckBalance` by full X but `bridgeAdjustment`\n        // only by net (= X - fee), so the baseline only grows from bridge activity. Plus\n        // yield accrual. Underflow would indicate corrupted state or wOToken depeg\n        // beyond expected magnitudes.\n        require(yieldOnly >= 0, \"Remote: negative yield baseline\");\n        bytes memory ackPayload = CrossChainV3Helper\n            .encodeBalanceCheckResponsePayload(\n                uint256(yieldOnly),\n                srcTimestamp\n            );\n        _sendYieldMessage(\n            CrossChainV3Helper.BALANCE_CHECK_RESPONSE,\n            nonce,\n            ackPayload\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Subtracts the snapshot Master sent (NOT `= 0`). Rationale:\n    ///\n    ///      At Remote-processing time, Remote.bridgeAdjustment may equal Master's snapshot\n    ///      (no in-flight ops), or differ by some delta (new bridge op has reached Remote\n    ///      between Master sending settle and Remote processing it). By subtracting only\n    ///      the exact snapshot, any newer delta is preserved on Remote \u2014 and Master does\n    ///      the symmetric subtract in `_processSettlementAck`, so both sides converge.\n    ///\n    ///      The reported balance is yield-only baseline (`_viewCheckBalance - bridgeAdj`\n    ///      post-subtract), so even if a new bridge op landed in between, the report is\n    ///      consistent with Master's reconstruction.\n    function _processSettlement(uint64 nonce, bytes memory body) internal {\n        int256 snapshot = abi.decode(body, (int256));\n        bridgeAdjustment -= snapshot;\n        int256 yieldOnly = int256(_viewCheckBalance()) - bridgeAdjustment;\n        require(yieldOnly >= 0, \"Remote: negative yield baseline\");\n        bytes memory ackPayload = CrossChainV3Helper.encodeNewBalancePayload(\n            uint256(yieldOnly)\n        );\n        _sendYieldMessage(\n            CrossChainV3Helper.SETTLE_BRIDGE_ACCOUNTING_ACK,\n            nonce,\n            ackPayload\n        );\n        _acceptYieldNonce(nonce);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Leg 1 of Option 1. Unwrap wOToken \u2192 OToken, request a withdrawal from the\n     *      Ethereum OToken vault queue, reply to Master with the new view of `checkBalance`.\n     *      Master doesn't need the `requestId` (Remote owns the queue lifecycle).\n     */\n    function _processWithdrawRequest(uint64 nonce, bytes memory payload)\n        internal\n    {\n        uint256 amount = CrossChainV3Helper.decodeAmountPayload(payload);\n        require(amount > 0, \"Remote: zero withdraw\");\n        require(outstandingRequestId == 0, \"Remote: queue already busy\");\n\n        // Unwrap wOToken \u2192 OToken to satisfy the queue request.\n        uint256 sharesNeeded = IERC4626(woToken).previewWithdraw(amount);\n        require(\n            IERC20(woToken).balanceOf(address(this)) >= sharesNeeded,\n            \"Remote: insufficient shares\"\n        );\n        IERC4626(woToken).withdraw(amount, address(this), address(this));\n\n        // Queue the withdrawal on the OToken vault. Allowance pre-granted by\n        // `safeApproveAllTokens`.\n        (uint256 requestId, ) = IVault(oTokenVault).requestWithdrawal(amount);\n        // slither-disable-next-line reentrancy-no-eth\n        outstandingRequestId = requestId;\n        queuedAmount = amount;\n        outstandingRequestAmount = amount;\n\n        // Reply to Master with the new total.\n        uint256 newBalance = _viewCheckBalance();\n        bytes memory ackPayload = CrossChainV3Helper.encodeNewBalancePayload(\n            newBalance\n        );\n        _sendYieldMessage(\n            CrossChainV3Helper.WITHDRAW_REQUEST_ACK,\n            nonce,\n            ackPayload\n        );\n        _acceptYieldNonce(nonce);\n\n        emit WithdrawRequestProcessed(nonce, amount, requestId);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Leg 2 of Option 1. If the OToken-vault queue hasn't been claimed yet, try the\n     *      claim opportunistically. If the bridgeAsset is in hand, bridge it back to Master.\n     *      Otherwise reply with a NACK so Master can retry later.\n     */\n    function _processWithdrawClaim(uint64 nonce) internal {\n        // Best-effort claim (idempotent \u2014 early-returns if already claimed).\n        _opportunisticClaim();\n\n        // The originally-requested amount caps what leg-2 may ship \u2014 residual bridgeAsset\n        // (donations, leftover from prior flows) stays on Remote rather than getting\n        // attributed to this withdrawal. `outstandingRequestAmount` is refined to the\n        // actually-claimed amount inside `_opportunisticClaim` if the queue paid out.\n        uint256 target = outstandingRequestAmount;\n        uint256 bridgeAssetHeld = IERC20(bridgeAsset).balanceOf(address(this));\n\n        if (target == 0 || bridgeAssetHeld < target) {\n            // Not ready (claim hasn't landed yet) or no outstanding request: NACK.\n            uint256 currentBalance = _viewCheckBalance();\n            bytes memory nackPayload = CrossChainV3Helper\n                .encodeWithdrawClaimAckPayload(currentBalance, false, 0);\n            _sendYieldMessage(\n                CrossChainV3Helper.WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK,\n                nonce,\n                nackPayload\n            );\n            _acceptYieldNonce(nonce);\n            emit WithdrawClaimNack(nonce, currentBalance);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        uint256 amount = target;\n\n        // Clear queue-side state (will be re-set if a fresh leg 1 starts) and bridge back.\n        queuedAmount = 0;\n        // slither-disable-next-line reentrancy-no-eth\n        outstandingRequestId = 0;\n        outstandingRequestAmount = 0;\n\n        uint256 newBalance = _viewCheckBalance() - amount; // bridgeAsset about to leave us\n        bytes memory ackPayload = CrossChainV3Helper\n            .encodeWithdrawClaimAckPayload(newBalance, true, amount);\n        // bridgeAsset \u2192 outboundAdapter allowance is granted by `setOutboundAdapter`.\n        _sendYieldTokensAndMessage(\n            bridgeAsset,\n            amount,\n            CrossChainV3Helper.WITHDRAW_CLAIM_ACK,\n            nonce,\n            ackPayload\n        );\n        _acceptYieldNonce(nonce);\n\n        emit WithdrawClaimDelivered(nonce, amount, newBalance);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @notice Permissionless, idempotent: claim the outstanding queue withdrawal if its delay\n     *         has elapsed. Safe to call multiple times \u2014 early-returns when nothing's pending.\n     */\n    function claimRemoteWithdrawal() external nonReentrant {\n        _opportunisticClaim();\n    }\n\n    function _opportunisticClaim() internal {\n        uint256 id = outstandingRequestId;\n        if (id == 0) {\n            return;\n        }\n        // Hoist `claimed` outside the try so its scope is unambiguous to static\n        // analysers (avoids the slither uninitialized-local false-positive that\n        // fired when `claimed` was named only in the try-returns clause).\n        uint256 claimed;\n        // Use try/catch so a not-yet-claimable queue delay doesn't bubble up as a revert.\n        try IVault(oTokenVault).claimWithdrawal(id) returns (uint256 _claimed) {\n            claimed = _claimed;\n            // slither-disable-next-line reentrancy-no-eth\n            outstandingRequestId = 0;\n            queuedAmount = 0;\n            // Refine `outstandingRequestAmount` to what the vault actually paid out so\n            // leg-2 ships the precise claimed amount (accounts for any rounding gain/loss\n            // between request time and claim time).\n            outstandingRequestAmount = claimed;\n            emit RemoteWithdrawalClaimed(id, claimed);\n        } catch {\n            // Still queued; leave state unchanged.\n        }\n    }\n\n    function _processYieldDeposit(uint64 nonce, uint256 amount) internal {\n        // bridgeAsset already arrived with the tokens-with-message delivery. Mint OToken\n        // from the Ethereum vault, then wrap to wOToken.\n        require(\n            IERC20(bridgeAsset).balanceOf(address(this)) >= amount,\n            \"Remote: deposit asset missing\"\n        );\n\n        // Mint OToken via the yield-side vault. The real OUSD / OETH vault pulls\n        // bridgeAsset via transferFrom inside `mint`; allowance pre-granted by\n        // `safeApproveAllTokens`.\n        IVault(oTokenVault).mint(amount);\n\n        // Whatever OToken we now hold gets wrapped to wOToken (allowance pre-granted).\n        uint256 oTokenBalance = IERC20(oToken).balanceOf(address(this));\n        if (oTokenBalance > 0) {\n            IERC4626(woToken).deposit(oTokenBalance, address(this));\n        }\n\n        // Reply to Master with the new balance and mark the yield nonce processed.\n        uint256 newBalance = _viewCheckBalance();\n        bytes memory ackPayload = CrossChainV3Helper.encodeNewBalancePayload(\n            newBalance\n        );\n        _sendYieldMessage(CrossChainV3Helper.DEPOSIT_ACK, nonce, ackPayload);\n        _acceptYieldNonce(nonce);\n\n        emit YieldDepositProcessed(nonce, amount, newBalance);\n    }\n\n    // --- AbstractWOTokenStrategy hooks -------------------------------------\n\n    /// @inheritdoc AbstractWOTokenStrategy\n    function _bridgeOutboundMsgType() internal pure override returns (uint32) {\n        return CrossChainV3Helper.BRIDGE_IN;\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc AbstractWOTokenStrategy\n    /// @dev Remote can always wrap user-supplied OToken; no liquidity check needed.\n    function _preflightBridgeOutbound(uint256) internal view override {}\n\n    /// @inheritdoc AbstractWOTokenStrategy\n    function _consumeOTokenForBridge(uint256 amount) internal override {\n        // Pull OToken from the user and wrap into wOToken shares held by this strategy.\n        IERC20(oToken).safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), amount);\n        IERC4626(woToken).deposit(amount, address(this));\n    }\n\n    /// @inheritdoc AbstractWOTokenStrategy\n    function _deliverOTokenForBridge(uint256 amount, address recipient)\n        internal\n        override\n    {\n        // Defensive: ensure we actually hold enough OToken value to satisfy this bridge-out.\n        uint256 sharesNeeded = IERC4626(woToken).previewWithdraw(amount);\n        require(\n            IERC20(woToken).balanceOf(address(this)) >= sharesNeeded,\n            \"Remote: insufficient remote wOToken\"\n        );\n\n        IERC4626(woToken).withdraw(amount, address(this), address(this));\n        IERC20(oToken).safeTransfer(recipient, amount);\n    }\n\n    // --- Helpers -----------------------------------------------------------\n\n    function _viewCheckBalance() internal view returns (uint256) {\n        // Value lives in exactly one slot at any time per the state-transition table:\n        //   - shares  (4626 wrapped)\n        //   - oToken  (unwrapped but not yet queued / redeemed)\n        //   - bridgeAsset (claimed / redeemed but not yet bridged back)\n        //   - queuedAmount (sitting in OToken-vault queue)\n        uint256 sharesBalance = IERC20(woToken).balanceOf(address(this));\n        uint256 valueOfShares = sharesBalance == 0\n            ? 0\n            : IERC4626(woToken).previewRedeem(sharesBalance);\n        return\n            valueOfShares +\n            IERC20(oToken).balanceOf(address(this)) +\n            IERC20(bridgeAsset).balanceOf(address(this)) +\n            queuedAmount;\n    }\n}\n","keccak256":"0x87f865b0581fd459cd1ef85ff8382796a0cb8759b1e70b5d303aacaff8382249","license":"BUSL-1.1"},"contracts/token/OUSD.sol":{"content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\npragma solidity ^0.8.0;\n\n/**\n * @title OUSD Token Contract\n * @dev ERC20 compatible contract for OUSD\n * @dev Implements an elastic supply\n * @author Origin Protocol Inc\n */\nimport { IVault } from \"../interfaces/IVault.sol\";\nimport { Governable } from \"../governance/Governable.sol\";\nimport { SafeCast } from \"@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/math/SafeCast.sol\";\n\ncontract OUSD is Governable {\n    using SafeCast for int256;\n    using SafeCast for uint256;\n\n    /// @dev Event triggered when the supply changes\n    /// @param totalSupply Updated token total supply\n    /// @param rebasingCredits Updated token rebasing credits\n    /// @param rebasingCreditsPerToken Updated token rebasing credits per token\n    event TotalSupplyUpdatedHighres(\n        uint256 totalSupply,\n        uint256 rebasingCredits,\n        uint256 rebasingCreditsPerToken\n    );\n    /// @dev Event triggered when an account opts in for rebasing\n    /// @param account Address of the account\n    event AccountRebasingEnabled(address account);\n    /// @dev Event triggered when an account opts out of rebasing\n    /// @param account Address of the account\n    event AccountRebasingDisabled(address account);\n    /// @dev Emitted when `value` tokens are moved from one account `from` to\n    ///      another `to`.\n    /// @param from Address of the account tokens are moved from\n    /// @param to Address of the account tokens are moved to\n    /// @param value Amount of tokens transferred\n    event Transfer(address indexed from, address indexed to, uint256 value);\n    /// @dev Emitted when the allowance of a `spender` for an `owner` is set by\n    ///      a call to {approve}. `value` is the new allowance.\n    /// @param owner Address of the owner approving allowance\n    /// @param spender Address of the spender allowance is granted to\n    /// @param value Amount of tokens spender can transfer\n    event Approval(\n        address indexed owner,\n        address indexed spender,\n        uint256 value\n    );\n    /// @dev Yield resulting from {changeSupply} that a `source` account would\n    ///      receive is directed to `target` account.\n    /// @param source Address of the source forwarding the yield\n    /// @param target Address of the target receiving the yield\n    event YieldDelegated(address source, address target);\n    /// @dev Yield delegation from `source` account to the `target` account is\n    ///      suspended.\n    /// @param source Address of the source suspending yield forwarding\n    /// @param target Address of the target no longer receiving yield from `source`\n    ///        account\n    event YieldUndelegated(address source, address target);\n\n    enum RebaseOptions {\n        NotSet,\n        StdNonRebasing,\n        StdRebasing,\n        YieldDelegationSource,\n        YieldDelegationTarget\n    }\n\n    uint256[154] private _gap; // Slots to align with deployed contract\n    uint256 private constant MAX_SUPPLY = type(uint128).max;\n    /// @dev The amount of tokens in existence\n    uint256 public totalSupply;\n    mapping(address => mapping(address => uint256)) private allowances;\n    /// @dev The vault with privileges to execute {mint}, {burn}\n    ///     and {changeSupply}\n    address public vaultAddress;\n    mapping(address => uint256) internal creditBalances;\n    // the 2 storage variables below need trailing underscores to not name collide with public functions\n    uint256 private rebasingCredits_; // Sum of all rebasing credits (creditBalances for rebasing accounts)\n    uint256 private rebasingCreditsPerToken_;\n    /// @dev The amount of tokens that are not rebasing - receiving yield\n    uint256 public nonRebasingSupply;\n    mapping(address => uint256) internal alternativeCreditsPerToken;\n    /// @dev A map of all addresses and their respective RebaseOptions\n    mapping(address => RebaseOptions) public rebaseState;\n    mapping(address => uint256) private __deprecated_isUpgraded;\n    /// @dev A map of addresses that have yields forwarded to. This is an\n    ///      inverse mapping of {yieldFrom}\n    /// Key Account forwarding yield\n    /// Value Account receiving yield\n    mapping(address => address) public yieldTo;\n    /// @dev A map of addresses that are receiving the yield. This is an\n    ///      inverse mapping of {yieldTo}\n    /// Key Account receiving yield\n    /// Value Account forwarding yield\n    mapping(address => address) public yieldFrom;\n\n    uint256 private constant RESOLUTION_INCREASE = 1e9;\n    uint256[34] private __gap; // including below gap totals up to 200\n\n    /// @dev Verifies that the caller is the Governor or Strategist.\n    modifier onlyGovernorOrStrategist() {\n        require(\n            isGovernor() || msg.sender == IVault(vaultAddress).strategistAddr(),\n            \"Caller is not the Strategist or Governor\"\n        );\n        _;\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Initializes the contract and sets necessary variables.\n    /// @param _vaultAddress Address of the vault contract\n    /// @param _initialCreditsPerToken The starting rebasing credits per token.\n    function initialize(address _vaultAddress, uint256 _initialCreditsPerToken)\n        external\n        onlyGovernor\n    {\n        require(_vaultAddress != address(0), \"Zero vault address\");\n        require(vaultAddress == address(0), \"Already initialized\");\n\n        rebasingCreditsPerToken_ = _initialCreditsPerToken;\n        vaultAddress = _vaultAddress;\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Returns the symbol of the token, a shorter version\n    ///      of the name.\n    function symbol() external pure virtual returns (string memory) {\n        return \"OUSD\";\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Returns the name of the token.\n    function name() external pure virtual returns (string memory) {\n        return \"Origin Dollar\";\n    }\n\n    /// @dev Returns the number of decimals used to get its user representation.\n    function decimals() external pure virtual returns (uint8) {\n        return 18;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @dev Verifies that the caller is the Vault contract\n     */\n    modifier onlyVault() {\n        require(vaultAddress == msg.sender, \"Caller is not the Vault\");\n        _;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return High resolution rebasingCreditsPerToken\n     */\n    function rebasingCreditsPerTokenHighres() external view returns (uint256) {\n        return rebasingCreditsPerToken_;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return
